data.title: MIE Holdings Reports Asset Impairment in 2024 Annual Update
data.description:
An update from MIE Holdings Corp. ( (HK:1555) ) is now available.

MIE Holdings Corporation has announced supplementary information regarding its 2024 Annual Report, focusing on impairment losses on assets. The company conducted an impairment assessment of its cash-generating unit (CGU) at the Daan Oilfield, using a discounted cash flow model to estimate the present value of future cash flows. The assessment revealed impairment losses of approximately RMB93.4 million, attributed mainly to oil and gas properties. The valuation was based on key assumptions such as crude oil prices, production volumes, and discount rates, reflecting a shorter forecast period due to the impending expiration of the production sharing contract in 2028.

More about MIE Holdings Corp.

MIE Holdings Corporation is engaged in the exploration, development, production, and sale of crude oil and other petroleum products, primarily operating under the Daan Oilfield in the People’s Republic of China. The company operates under a production sharing contract with China National Petroleum Corporation, which is set to expire on February 29, 2028.

data.title: Transport International Announces Scrip Dividend Scheme for 2025 Interim Dividend
data.description:
Transport International Holdings Limited ( (HK:0062) ) has issued an update.

Transport International Holdings Limited has announced a Scrip Dividend Scheme for its interim dividend for the six months ending on June 30, 2025. Shareholders can choose to receive their dividend in cash, new shares, or a combination of both. The market value for the new shares is set at HK$10.54, based on the average closing price over a specified period. This scheme offers flexibility to shareholders and may impact the company’s share distribution and liquidity. Eligible shareholders must submit their election forms by October 8, 2025, and the new shares are expected to be listed on October 22, 2025.

data.title: Tat Hong Clarifies Purchase Agreement with China Nuclear Industry
data.description:
Tat Hong Equipment Service Co. Ltd. ( (HK:2153) ) has issued an update.

Tat Hong Equipment Service Co., Ltd. has clarified the nature and payment terms of its Purchase Agreement with China Nuclear Industry Huaxing Construction Co., Ltd. The agreement involves the procurement of machines to support the company’s ongoing projects, with a total value of RMB350 million. The payment terms include an offset mechanism where 30% of lease service fees receivable will be used to offset the equipment purchase, amounting to approximately RMB42 million. The company anticipates that the offset will be completed over four to five years, with the remaining balance covered by future projects. This arrangement is expected to optimize cash flow and does not obligate China Nuclear Industry to increase the offset ratio or provide additional collateral.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2153) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Tat Hong Equipment Service Co. Ltd. stock, see the HK:2153 Stock Forecast page.
